mission.title: headlamp: Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory requests and Limits?
mission.description: Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory requests and Limits?. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current headlamp deployment
steps.description:
Verify your headlamp version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
helm list -n headlamp 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working headlamp installation.

steps.title: Review headlamp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant headlamp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
kubectl get configmap -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=headlamp
```
Hi,

is it planned to implement real time metrics, so that the user can see what has been set as request and limit (CPU and memory) per pod and how much is utilized in %?

I think that would be a great addition, if not already present and I missed a

steps.title: Apply the fix for Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory…
steps.description:
Hi  . We do show metrics if the metrics-server is installed in the cluster which does show the current utilization. Can you check if this is what you mean?
We're also working on time based charts, powered by Prometheus.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
kubectl get events -n headlamp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Getting real time mertrics on Pod level for CPU/Memory…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi  . We do show metrics if the metrics-server is installed in the cluster which does show the current utilization. Can you check if this is what you mean?
We're also working on time based charts, powered by Prometheus.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with headlamp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
